what_it_does: Defines a one-week definition-of-done for a PWA feature set, describing the exact mobile behaviors that must work for a single user and the scoping discipline required to ship it.
observations: The item is titled 'P15 · One-week definition of done' and belongs to the dragnet-development channel
It targets a user named Tim performing a daily cycle on a PWA added to his home screen
It lists five concrete interaction scenarios: launch/top-bar, comment creation, immediate vs. queued submission, reply badge/reading, and document switching
It explicitly rejects emoji icon buttons and redundant on-screen echo of selections
It ends with a 'smallest sharp thing' framing and a 'not now' shipping discipline
It links to board://item-f9f4a6a6 via a part_of relationship
standouts: The definition of done is compressed to one week and a single user's closed daily loop
It explicitly says 'no emojis' and 'no redundant on-screen echo' as UX constraints
It reframes the rest of the brief as a roadmap of options to be evaluated only after real use
concerns: The acceptance criteria mix user story, UX details, and product strategy without separating must-have from nice-to-have
There are no explicit failure criteria, platform version constraints, or success metrics
It assumes an iOS/Safari PWA context without naming the platform
notes: A concise mobile-PWA definition-of-done note that anchors scope around one user's closed daily loop and resists roadmap expansion.
questions: Who is Tim and what user segment does he represent?
What is P15 and which product area does it cover?
What is the content and role of the parent item board://item-f9f4a6a6?
Which exact platform, browser, and PWA capabilities are assumed?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose and content align: it is exactly a one-week definition of done for a mobile PWA workflow.
intention_signals: Set a tight, user-scoped acceptance test for a PWA so the team ships a working daily instrument before considering any broader roadmap options.
novelty: Frames the definition of done as a one-week behavioral closure for a single named user, and explicitly uses 'not now' as a product discipline rather than a backlog state.
contribution: A concise, user-grounded definition-of-done statement that anchors scope decisions and prioritizes a closed mobile interaction loop.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard item defining a one-week definition of done for a PWA, centered on a single user's daily mobile comment-and-reply loop and a 'not now' scoping discipline.
intention_for_embedding: To align the team on the smallest shippable PWA behavior set before evaluating broader roadmap options.
